Items.Title: 分体式机械键盘，您可以自己组装！ | A Split Mechanical Keyboard, You Can Build Yourself!
Items.Description:
自从我开始在家工作以来，我一直痴迷于为自己创造最符合人体工程学的工作场所。 站立式办公桌、符合人体工学的凳子等。

但到目前为止，我无法合理地构建自己。 但一个月前，一切都变了。 我读到了拆分键盘的好处。 中间切开的键盘可防止您的手腕向内倾斜并放松您的手臂和肩膀。

我发现了一个充满热情的制作者的庞大社区： r/ErgoMechKeyboards 、许多视频和 Github 存储库，其中包含令人兴奋的设计供您复制和自己动手做。 在这个故事中，我总结了迄今为止收集到的所有知识，并将引导您完成创建键盘的过程。

你需要什么技能来创造这些美女之一？

很多，其实！ 它的范围应该从电子到 3D 建模再到焊接和购买东西。 最重要的是，这不是一个昂贵的项目。 它应该在 50 美元到 150 美元之间。 但是，如果您总体上对工程充满热情或好奇，那么这是一个出色的第一个项目，因为任何人都可以访问它。

主要活动有：

  1. 设计和印刷 PCB（印刷电路板）
  2. 购买和组装电子元件
  3. 设计和制造外壳以保护电子设备和外观冷却器😎。

这就是PCB的样子。


渡轮扫描 PCB 由 Pierre Viseu Chevalier、David Barr、Ibnu Daru Aji、Duccio 提供

要创建键盘，您基本上有两个选择：

  • 第二种方法有几个优点。 第一次焊接要容易得多； 您只需在板上创建的插座中焊接钥匙开关。 电路板本身足够坚固，可以握住按键并使键盘无需外壳即可使用。 因此，您可以创建一个超轻键盘，并在制作过程中节省一些精力，只需几美元。

    第一步是选择键盘的外观。 几把钥匙？ 键之间的间距等。

    在此处比较现有的拆分键盘布局。 作为第一步，查看可用的内容并打印布局以获得键盘的真实感受。

    我打印 Corne 的布局

    我想让您反思一下，您很可能只经常使用键盘上的一半键。 标准键盘通常只有 100 个以上的键。 您可能已经注意到，这些键盘中的大多数很少有超过 50 个键，这很好。

    使用拆分键盘的人键入的方式与您在手机键盘上键入的方式相同：首先，您只有字母，然后按“？123”符号，这样您就可以键入数字和一些符号； 然后，如果您想要更多符号，请键入“=<”键。

    最终，您将对键盘的布局进行编程，以便您可以做任何您想做的事情。

    一旦您确定了布局，您就可以对其进行自定义。 在 Google 或 Github 上搜索源以开始使用。 从理论上讲，您可以下载文件并将其发送到 PCB 制造。 但是，为什么要抓住机会拥有真正独特的键盘呢？

    我从这个视频中学习了有关如何定制 PCB 的基础知识。 我强烈推荐观看。 但这里是您需要的快速摘要。

    为了这个例子，我下载了Sweepv2.1 的 PCB Gerber 文件。


    查看右侧的不同层。 在存档中，您打开容器，每层有一个文件。

    • 该层通常为绿色，并为 PCB 提供独特的颜色。 幸运的是有更多颜色可供选择😉。
    • 好吧，这并不完全正确。 一旦您了解了每一层的用途，没有什么能阻止您根据自己的喜好定制 PCB 👼。 在编辑板之前，您需要知道要放入什么组件，这就是下一节的对象😈。

      您需要知道的第一件事是基本上有三种类型的机械开关。

      • 您与机械键盘关联的典型开关在敲击结束时会为您提供令人满意的咔嗒声。
      • 触觉开关没有那么嘈杂，但在您按下按键时会为您提供触觉反馈。
      • 线性开关越来越普遍，在您打字时不会提供反馈或噪音，旨在提供流畅和舒缓的打字体验。


      机械开关的种类

      其次，您可以发现高调与低调的细微差别。 这种选择通常与选择 Cherry MX VS 相关。 凯尔巧克力。

      大多数制造商都提供了这两种类型的配置文件，但我注意到在开源构建中，Kailh 被选为他们的低调键，而 Cherry MX 被选为他们的高调键。


      高调与低调由 u/songcq 提供

      选择应受您在键盘中寻找的感觉以及您是否希望键盘紧凑的影响。 对于紧凑型键盘，您需要选择低调的开关。

      这个 Github 存储库包含大多数制造商的数据表： https ://github.com/keyboardio/keyswitch_documentation

      您需要验证您选择的按键开关是否与您的 PCB 兼容。 否则，您将需要进行一些编辑以确保它们正确匹配。


      唯一重要的是选择与您选择的品牌相匹配的按键开关（Cherry MX VS. Kailh Chocolate）。 除此之外，尽情玩乐并探索市场上的众多选择。


      如果您想进一步推动这个项目，您可以决定打印自己的键帽。

      我在 Yeggi 上搜索 3D 模型

      微控制器是您键盘的大脑。 它通过实现键盘协议将击键转换为您的 PC 使用的可理解代码。

      幸运的是，感谢社区和开源键盘固件QMK ，您不必编写太多代码。 例如，使用 QMK，您只需使用在线配置器定义键盘映射，然后刷新微控制器。

      最受欢迎的选项是：

      • 微控制器板可能是此构建中最昂贵的元素，特别是考虑到您需要其中两个，因此您可以选择加入板载键盘。 我列出的控制器板基本上依赖于ATMEGA32U4 。 如果您有技能和知识，您可以考虑进行SMT 组装并将您的微控制器焊接在板上。

        微控制器板的插座。 假设您想将键盘转换为无线键盘，稍后插座将允许您


      微控制器板的插座

      • 热插拔钥匙开关插座。 这将允许您随意更改按键开关，并防止您将按键开关焊接到板上


      只是为了让事情变得花哨


    来自 crkbd 的 Corne 拆分键盘

    • 这样你就可以在键盘上显示


    帐篷支持。 帐篷包括在键盘上添加一些倾斜度，以便您的手腕具有更自然的位置


带帐篷的分体式键盘

我相信我们可以为键盘添加更多功能😆。

最后但同样重要的是，键盘的保护和装饰信封。

有些人喜欢 PCB 的外观并选择简单地切割亚克力板。 这个过程很简单，不需要加工，所以它是最好的选择之一。 最重要的是，如果您选择 LED，这肯定看起来很棒。


来自 littlekeyboards.com 的 Corne Technician 键盘保护套

第二种选择是拥有一个 3D 打印的外壳。 最好的办法是从现有设计开始并对其进行调整。

所有零件在哪里获得？

设计好键盘后，您可能想知道：如何获得所有部件？

使用 Gerber 文件，您可以打印 PCB：

  • 对于组件，网上有很多专门的商店：

    • 对于外壳和其他 3D 打印对象：

      • 结论

        我即将订购所有零件，因此我保留此部分以分享构建😉。

        这是我购买的零件的后续文章（每个键盘约 40 美元，不包括运费）：

        参考

        比较所有拆分键盘布局： https ://jhelvy.shinyapps.io/splitkbcompare/

        更多PCB编辑工具：https: //support.jlcpcb.com/article/22-how-to-generate-the-gerber-files

        开源键盘——Corne 线

        • 开源键盘 — GergoPlex Line

          • 开源键盘 — Ferries Line

            开源键盘——3W6 Line

            • 开源键盘——fifi 行

              • 其他文章：

                • https://switchandclick.com/are-ergonomic-keyboards-worth-it/ https://switchandclick.com/are-ergonomic-keyboards-worth-it/


原文:


Ferris split keyboard courtesy of Pierre Viseu Chevalier

Since I started working from home, I have been obsessed with creating the most ergonomic workplace for myself. Standing desks, ergonomic stools, etc.

But up until now, nothing I could reasonably build myself. 😢

But a month ago, everything changed. I read about the benefit of split keyboards. A Keyboard cut halfway through in the middle prevents your wrists from tilting inward and relaxing your arms and shoulders.

I discovered a massive community of passionate makers: r/ErgoMechKeyboards , many videos ,and Github repositories with exciting designs to copy and do yourself. ✨

In this Story, I have summarized all the knowledge I have gathered so far and will walk you through creating your Keyboard.

What skills do you need to create one of these beauties?

Quite a lot, actually! It should range from electronic to 3D modeling to soldering and buying stuff. On top of that, it is not an expensive project; it should range from $50 to $150. But if you are passionate or curious about engineering in general, it is an excellent first project as it is accessible by anyone.

The main activities are:

  1. Designing and Printing a PCB (Printed Circuit Board)
  2. Buying and Assembling the electronic components
  3. Designing and manufacturing the casing to protect the electronic and look event cooler 😎.

I) Designing and Printing a PCB

This is what a PCB looks like.


Ferries Sweep PCB courtesy of Pierre Viseu Chevalier, David Barr, Ibnu Daru Aji, Duccio

To create a keyboard, you essentially have two options:

  • 1) You create a case and mechanically attach the key switches to the case and solder cables between the switches.
  • 2) Print a PCB and solder the keys and other components.

There are several advantages to the second approach. First soldering is much easier; you just have to solder key switches in the sockets created on the board. The board itself is strong enough to hold the keys and make the Keyboard usable without a case. As a result, you can create an ultra-light keyboard and save you some effort in the process of making it for a few $.

I.1) Choosing the layout

The first step is choosing how your Keyboard will look like. How many keys? The spacing between the keys, etc.

Compare existing split keyboard layouts here. As a first step, see what is available and print the layout to get a real feel of the Keyboard.

☑️ Screen and Print keybord layouts from: https://jhelvy.shinyapps.io/splitkbcompare/


Me printing the layout of a Corne

I'd like you to reflect on the fact that you are most likely only frequently using half of the keys on your Keyboard. A standard keyboard usually has just above 100 keys. You may have noticed that most of those keyboards rarely have more than 50 keys, and this is perfectly fine.

People using split Keyboard type the same way you type on your phone keyboard: At first, you only have the letters, then you press the "?123" symbol so you can type number and some of the symbols; then, if you want more symbols you type the "=<" key.

Ultimately you will program the layout of your Keyboard so you will be able to do whatever you want.

⚠️Not all layout on https://jhelvy.shinyapps.io/splitkbcompare/ are open source, so you won’t be able to get the file for printing the PCB

I.2) Designing the PCB

Once you have set yourself to mind a layout, you can customize it. Search on Google or on Github for the source to get started. Theoretically, you could just download the file and send it to the PCB manufacturing. But why tunning your chance to have a genuinely unique keyboard?

I learned the basics about how to customize a PCB from this video. I highly recommend watching it. But here is a quick summary of what you need.

☑️ You will need a software to edit the PCB such as KiCad: https://www.kicad.org

For the sake of this example, I downloaded the PCB Gerber File for Sweepv2.1.


Sweepv2.1 in KiCad Gerber Viewer

❗️️️️❗️ ️️️️️If you want to have a quike look to a Gerber file archive you can use this Online Gerber Viewer (I found it much clearer to understand the different layers)

PCB manufacturers literally use Gerber Files to print your order layer bay layer. See the different layers on the right side. In the archive, you open containers with one file per layer.

  • *.xln is the Drill layer, = Where to make holes.
  • *.gm1 the board Edges = how to cut the PCB to detached from the mainboard.
  • *B_Cu.gbl and *F_Cu.gtl are the front and back copper layer = where to print copper wire to create connectivity.
  • *B_Mask.gts and *F_Mask.gbs are the back and front solder masks = was to print the protective layer on top of the copper layer. This layer is ofter green and gives there distinctive color to the PCB. Luckily more colors are available 😉.
  • \ **B_Silk.gbo* and \ **F_Silk.gto* are the back and front silkscreen = the decorative prints on top of the solder masks, usually used to write references dor the component to solder.

Well, this is not entirely true. Once you understand each layer's use, nothing stops you from customizing a PCB to your heart content 👼. Before editing the board, you need to know what component you want to fit in, and this is the object of the next section 😈.

II) Choosing the components

II.1) Choosing the switches

The first thing you need to know is that there are essentially three types of mechanical switches.

  • Clicky switches are the noisy ones. The typical switches you associate with a mechanical keyboard give you this satisfying click sound at the end of a stroke.
  • Tactile switches are not as noisy but give you tactile feedback when you press the key.
  • Linear switches are more and more common and provide no feedback or noise as you type, design for a smooth and soothing typing experience.


Types of mechanical switches

Second the nuance you can find high vs. low profile. This choice is often associated with choosing Cherry MX VS. Kailh Chocolate.

❗️️️️ ❗️️️️ T️here is more brand of key switches I invite you to read more about it here. Most manufacturer provided both type of profile, but i notice that in Open-source built Kailh was choosen for their low profile key and Cherry MX for their high profile key.


High Profile vs. Low Profile courtesy of u/songcq

The choice should be influenced by the feeling you are looking for in your Keyboard and whether you want your Keyboard to be compact or not. For a compact Keyboard, you need to go for low-profile switches.

☑️ You will need to select you switches and the download the specs (datasheet). This Github repository contains datasheets for most manufacturer: https://github.com/keyboardio/keyswitch_documentation

You need to validate that the key switches you are selecting are compatible with your PCB. Otherwise, you will need to do some edits to make sure they properly fit.


Circuit Board Layout for Keith Choc Blue

II.2) Choosing the keycaps

The only thing that matters is selecting key switches that match the brand you choose (Cherry MX VS. Kailh Chocolate). Aside from that, just have fun and explore the many options available on the market.


Fun Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboard from Etsy

If you want to push this project a bit further, you can decide to print your own keycap.

☑️ You can screen for existing 3D model with online libraries like : https://www.yeggi.com/


Me searching for 3D Models on Yeggi

II.3) Choosing the micro-controller

The microcontroller est the brain for your keyboard. It converts keystrokes into understandable code used by your PC by implementing the Keyboard Protocol.

Luckily you won’t have to code to much thanks to the community and the Open-source keyboard firmware QMK. With QMK you only need to define the keymap using an online configurator, for instance, then flash the microcontroller.

The most popular options are:

  • Pro Micro most common microcontroller board, you are bringing Arduino’s power to your board.
  • Elite-C higher-end microcontroller, ideal if you plan to add features over time.
  • Nice!nano if you want to create a vluthouth keyboard

A microcontroller board might be the most expensive element of this build especially given that you need two of them so you could opt-in for onboard keyboards. The controller board I listed essentially relies on ATMEGA32U4. If you have the skills and knowledge you could consider going for SMT assembly and have your microcontroller solder on the board.

II.4) Extra designed feature you may want to consider

  • Socket for the microcontroller board. Let's say you want to convert the keyboard into a wireless keyboard later socket would allow you to


Socket for the microcontroller board

  • Hot-swappable key switches socket. This would allow you to change the key switches at will and prevent you from having to solder the key switches to the board


Kailh Hot-Swap Sockets courtesy of u/davenelsondotcom

  • RGB LEDs. Just to make things fancy


Corne Split Keyboard from crkbd

  • OLED Arduino module. So you can have on the keyboard display


OLED Arduino module

  • Tenting support. Tenting consist of adding some inclination to the keyboard so your wrists have a more natural position


Split keyboard with Tenting

I am sure the is a lot more feature we can add to a keyboard 😆.

☑️ Select your features, study the componenents, make adjustement to your PCB.

III) Designing the case

Last but not least the case, the protective and decorative envelope for your keyboard.

Some people like the look of the PCB and choose to simply have cut acrylic sheets. The process is simple and requires no machining so it is one of the best options. On top of that if you opted for the LED this would for sure look awesome.


Corne Technician Keyboard Case from littlekeyboards.com

The second option is to have a 3D-printed case. The best would be to start from an existing design and adapt it.

☑️ You can screen for existing 3D model with online libraries like : https://www.yeggi.com/

Where to get all the parts?

Once you have designed your keyboard you may be wondering: how do I get all the parts?

With your Gerber file you can get your PCB printed with:

  • https://www.pcbway.com
  • https://jlcpcb.com

For the components there is a bunch of dedicated stores online:

  • http://mechboards.co.uk
  • http://splitkb.com

For the case and other 3D printed objects:

  • https://jlcpcb.com
  • https://i.materialise.com/

❗️️️️ ❗️️️️ Shipping fees seems to be the most expensive part of this build so try to find local manufacturer to reduce the costs.

Conclusion

I am about to order all the parts so I keep this section to share the build 😉.

Here is the follow-up article with the part I bought (about $40 per keyboard excluding shipping fees):

How to Build a Custom Split Mechanical Keyboard: Bill of material

References

Compare all split keyboard layouts: https://jhelvy.shinyapps.io/splitkbcompare/

More PCB editing tools: https://support.jlcpcb.com/article/22-how-to-generate-the-gerber-files

Opensource Keyboard — Corne lines

  • https://github.com/MakotoKurauchi/helix
  • https://github.com/foostan/crkbd

Opensource Keyboard — GergoPlex Line

  • https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TpJQtzaThmM

Opensource Keyboard — Ferries Line

Opensource Keyboard— 3W6 Line

  • https://github.com/weteor/3W6

Opensource Keyboard — fifi line

  • https://github.com/raychengy/fifi_split_keeb

Other articles:

  • https://switchandclick.com/are-ergonomic-keyboards-worth-it/

Items.Title: 构建接受环境变量的销售或仪表板泊坞窗图像 | Build saleor dashboard docker image that accept environment variables
Items.Description:
一个开源、GraphQL 优先的电子商务平台，提供超快速、动态和个性化的购物体验。

通常，我们作为开发人员使用销售团队提供的前 2 个组件，并在其上构建自定义店面

问题

例如，如果您有 4 个环境development 、 stagging 、 test和production ，那么您将需要构建 4 个不同版本的仪表板

我看了一圈，看看是否有人问过同样的问题，我发现了这个问题 #1592

我的解决方案

上述问题的唯一答案表明修改config.ts和index.html文件可以解决问题，所以我将解释如何手动完成，然后随意自动化该过程

获取仪表板代码

克隆 repo 并签出您需要构建的版本

git clone --depth 1 --branch <ref> https://github.com/saleor/saleor.git

配置文件

我们将创建一个 bash 脚本env.sh读取$API_URI变量并生成一个配置 javascript 文件，该脚本将在每次 docker 容器启动之前运行

请注意，docker 容器将从名为dashboard的文件夹中为仪表板提供服务，这就是脚本将在同一文件夹中生成 env 文件的原因

#!/bin/bash target_file = . / dashboard / env - config . js touch $target_file rm - rf $target_file echo " window._env_ = { " >> $target_file echo " API_URI: \" $API_URI \" , " >> $target_file echo " } " >> $target_file

生成的文件应该是这样的

window . _env_ = { API_URI : " <api-url-from-env> " }

修改src/index.html

编辑仪表板的主html文件，并在头部添加这个脚本标签

<!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <meta charset= "UTF-8" > <meta name= "viewport" content= "width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no" > <meta name= "robots" content= "noindex" > <title> Saleor e-commerce </title> <script src= "/dashboard/env-config.js" ></script> <!-- add this line--> </head> <body> <div id= "dashboard-app" ></div> </body> </html>

修改src/config.ts

现在我们将在运行时从window对象中读取API_URI ，而不是在构建时从环境中读取它

import packageInfo from " ../package.json " ; import { SearchVariables } from " ./hooks/makeSearch " ; import { ListSettings , ListViews , Pagination } from " ./types " ; export const APP_MOUNT_URI = process . env . APP_MOUNT_URI || " / " ; export const APP_DEFAULT_URI = " / " ; // remove this line export const API_URI = process . env . API_URI ; // add these 2 lines, typescript build would fail without the @ts-ignore // @ts-ignore export const API_URI = window . _env_ . API_URI ; .... ....

修改Dockerfile

对于 docker 文件，我们需要做两件事

  1. 将env.sh脚本复制到 docker 镜像根目录中

  2. 在启动容器之前更改命令以运行env.sh

FROM node:14 as builder WORKDIR /app COPY package*.json ./ RUN npm install COPY . . ARG APP_MOUNT_URI ARG API_URI ARG STATIC_URL ENV API_URI ${API_URI:-http://localhost:8000/graphql/} ENV APP_MOUNT_URI ${APP_MOUNT_URI:-/dashboard/} ENV STATIC_URL ${STATIC_URL:-/dashboard/} RUN STATIC_URL = ${ STATIC_URL } API_URI = ${ API_URI } APP_MOUNT_URI = ${ APP_MOUNT_URI } npm run build FROM nginx:stable WORKDIR /app COPY ./nginx/default.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf COPY --from=builder /app/build/ /app/ # Add the following lines COPY ./env.sh . RUN chmod +x env.sh CMD ["/bin/bash", "-c", "/app/env.sh && nginx -g \"daemon off;\""]

不，您可以构建从部署环境中获取API_URI saleor-dashboard图像

笔记

为了让我不要对仪表板的每个新版本重复上述步骤，我创建了一个 gitlab CI/CD 管道来自动为我指定的任何版本/标签/引用执行此操作，但不幸的是，这是我无法共享的



原文:

Saleor as mentioned in its landing page is

An open-source, GraphQL-first e-commerce platform delivering ultra-fast, dynamic and personalized shopping experiences.

Saleor is built as separate components

  • saleor-core which is the graphql api
  • saleor-dashboard which is the amdin area for the app
  • saleor-storefront which is the user facing part of the ecommerce platform

Usually, we as developers use the first 2 components as provided by the saleor team and build a custom storefront on top of it

The problem

The saleor-dashboard is a react app that is build as a static site in a docker container but to do that we have to provide the API_URI at the build time
so for example if you have 4 environment development, stagging, test and production then you will need to build 4 different versions of the dashboard

i looked a round to see if someone else asked the same question and i found this issue #1592

My solution

The only answer on the above mentioned issue suggested that modifying the config.ts and index.html file would do the trick, so i will explain how you can do it manually, then feel free to automate the process

Get the dashboard code

clone the repo and checkout the version you need to build

git clone --depth 1 --branch <ref>  https://github.com/saleor/saleor.git

Config file

we will create a bash script env.sh that read the $API_URI env variable and generate a config javascript file, this script will run every time before the docker container started

notice that the docker container will serve the dashboard from a folder called dashboard that is why the script will generate the env file in the same folder

#!/bin/bash
target_file=./dashboard/env-config.js touch $target_file
rm -rf $target_file echo "window._env_ = {" >> $target_file echo " API_URI: \"$API_URI\"," >> $target_file
echo "}" >> $target_file

The generated file should look like this

window._env_ = { API_URI: "<api-url-from-env>"
}

Modify src/index.html

Edit the main html file of the dashboard and add this script tag on the head

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no"> <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> <title>Saleor e-commerce</title> <script src="/dashboard/env-config.js"></script> <!-- add this line--> </head> <body> <div id="dashboard-app"></div> </body>
</html>

Modify src/config.ts

now we will read the API_URI from the window object at the runtime instead of reading it from environment at build time

import packageInfo from "../package.json";
import { SearchVariables } from "./hooks/makeSearch";
import { ListSettings, ListViews, Pagination } from "./types"; export const APP_MOUNT_URI = process.env.APP_MOUNT_URI || "/";
export const APP_DEFAULT_URI = "/";
// remove this line
export const API_URI = process.env.API_URI; // add these 2 lines, typescript build would fail without the @ts-ignore
// @ts-ignore
export const API_URI = window._env_.API_URI;
....
....

Modify Dockerfile

for the docker file we need to do 2 things

  1. copy the env.sh script into the docker image root

  2. change the command to run the env.sh before starting the container

FROM node:14 as builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm install
COPY . .
ARG APP_MOUNT_URI
ARG API_URI
ARG STATIC_URL
ENV API_URI ${API_URI:-http://localhost:8000/graphql/}
ENV APP_MOUNT_URI ${APP_MOUNT_URI:-/dashboard/}
ENV STATIC_URL ${STATIC_URL:-/dashboard/}
RUN STATIC_URL=${STATIC_URL} API_URI=${API_URI} APP_MOUNT_URI=${APP_MOUNT_URI} npm run build FROM nginx:stable
WORKDIR /app
COPY ./nginx/default.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
COPY --from=builder /app/build/ /app/
# Add the following lines
COPY ./env.sh .
RUN chmod +x env.sh
CMD ["/bin/bash", "-c", "/app/env.sh && nginx -g \"daemon off;\""]

No you can build saleor-dashboard image that take the API_URI from your deployment environment

Note

For me to not repeat the above steps with every new version of the dashboard, i created a gitlab CI/CD pipeline to do this automatically for whatever version/tag/ref i specify but unfortunately this is something i can't share

Items.Title: 使用 Redis 在 Node.js 中实现缓存 | Implement caching in Node.js with Redis
Items.Description:
萨尔阿姆拉尼

发表于缓存是一种简单的机制，可以让您的 API 更快地响应重复的请求。

让我们描述一下我们的问题：我们有一个简单的 API，可以从某个站点抓取一些数据，并对这些数据执行一些繁重的计算。 我们的 API 响应很慢 - 这对我们的用户来说不是很好。

我们知道这个特定的请求可能会收到很多次，并且我们抓取的网站中的信息只会每小时更新一次。

我们的解决方案：缓存！ 我们可以在接下来的一个小时内缓存我们的第一个响应，并避免一遍又一遍地进行那些缓慢的计算。

执行：
我假设你的机器上安装了 Redis - 如果你没有， Redis 文档非常容易理解。

首先让我们用 express 启动我们的应用程序

//index.js const express = require("express"); const app = express(); app.get("/heavy-task",(req, res) => { const { searchTerm } = req.query; const result = heavyTask(searchTerm); res.status(200).json({ result }); }); app.listen(PORT, () => { console.log("Server is listening on port " + PORT); });

现在让我们安装我们的 Redis 客户端以在我们的应用程序中使用 redis - 我将使用ioredis 。
我们将在一个名为cache.js的不同文件中启动我们的客户端

//cache.js const Redis = require("ioredis"); const redisClient = new Redis(); redisClient.on("connect", () => { console.log("Redis connected"); }); redisClient.on("error", (err) => { console.log("Redis error", err); }) module.exports = redisClient;

让我们将我们的 redisClient 导入index.js以在我们的处理程序上使用它。
我们将使用SETEX方法，它接受我们的数据存储密钥，一个数字，表示我们的数据将在存储中存在的秒数 - 最后，存储的数据为 JSON。

 //index.js const express = require("express"); const redisClient = require("./cache.js"); const app = express(); app.get("/heavy-task",(req, res) => { const { searchTerm } = req.query; const result = heavyTask(searchTerm); const resultJSON = JSON.stringify(result); redisClient.setex(searchTerm, 3600, resultJSON); res.status(200).json({ result }); }); app.listen(PORT, () => { console.log("Server is listening on port " + PORT); });

伟大的 ！ 现在我们的数据将在接下来的一个小时内存储在 Redis 中。

现在我们将创建我们的checkCache中间件，该中间件将在每次收到请求时运行，并且将简单地询问 Redis 是否存在searchTerm （键） - 如果存在则返回数据 - 否则next() 。

 //middleware/checkCache.js const redisClient = require("../cache.js"); const checkCache = (req, res, next) => { const { searchTerm } = req.query; redisClient.get(searchTerm, (err, result) => { if (err) { console.error(err); } if (result !== null) { const cachedRes = JSON.parse(result); return res.status(200).json(cachedRes); } else { next(); } }); }; module.exports = checkCache;

在处理程序中实现我们的中间件。

 //index.js const express = require("express"); const redisClient = require("./cache.js"); const checkCache = require("./middleware/checkCache.js"); const app = express(); app.get("/heavy-task",checkCache,(req, res) => { const { searchTerm } = req.query; const result = heavyTask(searchTerm); const resultJSON = JSON.stringify(result); redisClient.setex(searchTerm, 3600, resultJSON); res.status(200).json({ result }); }); app.listen(PORT, () => { console.log("Server is listening on port " + PORT); });

就是这样 ！ 现在我们的请求很容易被 Redis 缓存。

希望这个简单的指南对您有所帮助😄

Hemanshu Upadhyay - Hemanshu Upadhyay - Apr 10 4月10日



原文:

Saar Amrani

Posted on Apr 25

Caching is a simple mechanism that can make your API respond faster to a request that is repetitive.

Lets describe our problem: We have a simple API that scrape some data from a certain site and performs some heavy calculations on that data. Our API response is slow - and this is not great for our users.

We know that this specific request may be received many times and that the information in our scraped site will only update every hour.

Our solution: Caching ! We can cache our first response for the next hour and avoid doing those slow calculations over and over again.

Redis is a very fast in memory data store that is perfect for this kind of tasks.

Implementation:
I will assume that you have Redis installed on your machine - if you don't, the Redis docs are very easy and simple to understand.

First of all lets initiate our app with express

//index.js
const express = require("express");

const app = express();

app.get("/heavy-task",(req, res) => {
  const { searchTerm } = req.query;

  const result = heavyTask(searchTerm);

  res.status(200).json({ result });
});

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log("Server is listening on port " + PORT);
});

Now lets install our Redis client to use redis in our app - I'll be using ioredis.
We will initiate our client in a different file called cache.js

//cache.js

const Redis = require("ioredis");

const redisClient = new Redis();

redisClient.on("connect", () => {
  console.log("Redis connected");
});

redisClient.on("error", (err) => {
  console.log("Redis error", err);
})


module.exports = redisClient;

Lets import our redisClient to the index.js to use it on our handler.
We will use the SETEX method which accept our key for the data store, a number which represent the amount of seconds our data will live in the store - and lastly, the stored data as JSON.

//index.js
const express = require("express");
const redisClient = require("./cache.js");

const app = express();

app.get("/heavy-task",(req, res) => {
  const { searchTerm } = req.query;

  const result = heavyTask(searchTerm);

  const resultJSON =  JSON.stringify(result);

  redisClient.setex(searchTerm, 3600, resultJSON);

  res.status(200).json({ result });
});

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log("Server is listening on port " + PORT);
});

Great ! now our data will be stored in Redis for the next hour.

Now we will create our checkCache middleware which will run each time the request is received and will simply ask Redis if the searchTerm (key) exists - if so return the data - else next().

//middleware/checkCache.js
const redisClient = require("../cache.js");

const checkCache = (req, res, next) => {
  const { searchTerm } = req.query;
  redisClient.get(searchTerm, (err, result) => {
    if (err) {
      console.error(err);
    }
    if (result !== null) {
      const cachedRes = JSON.parse(result);
      return res.status(200).json(cachedRes);
    } else {
      next();
    }
  });
};

module.exports = checkCache;

Implement our middleware in the handler.

//index.js
const express = require("express");
const redisClient = require("./cache.js");
const checkCache = require("./middleware/checkCache.js");

const app = express();

app.get("/heavy-task",checkCache,(req, res) => {
  const { searchTerm } = req.query;

  const result = heavyTask(searchTerm);

  const resultJSON =  JSON.stringify(result);

  redisClient.setex(searchTerm, 3600, resultJSON);

  res.status(200).json({ result });
});

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log("Server is listening on port " + PORT);
});

Thats It ! now our request are easily cached with Redis.

Hope you found this simple guide helpful 😄

Hemanshu Upadhyay - Apr 10

Items.Title: 使用 HTML/CSS/JS 创建可翻转卡片 | Creating Flipable Card Using HTML/CSS/JS
Items.Description:
您可以通过跟踪这些步骤轻松创建可翻转卡片。

我们有两种类型的面孔。 正常面和翻转面。

我们的重要属性是backface-visibilty: hidden; 这个属性帮助我们隐藏元素的背面。

其他重要的属性是transform-style: preserve-3d; 此属性在元素旋转时为元素提供 3D 旋转效果。

和 JavaScript 文件：

 var card = document . querySelector ( ' .wordCard ' ); card . addEventListener ( ' click ' , () => { card . classList . toggle ( ' flipped ' ); });

在上面，我们设置click事件来切换.wordCard类。 通过这种方式，我们可以为这张卡片设置过渡持续时间。



原文:

You can create flipable card easily by tracking these steps.

HTML file:

<div class="wordCard"> <div class="cardFace frontFace"> <span>front</span> </div> <div class="cardFace backFace"> <span>back</span> </div>
</div>

CSS file:

.wordCard { width: 200px; height: 300px; position: relative; perspective: 1000px; cursor: pointer;
} .cardFace { display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; position: absolute; color: white; width: 100%; height: 100%; border-radius: 10px; backface-visibility: hidden; -webkit-backface-visibility: hidden; transition: transform .5s linear; transform-style: preserve-3d;
} .backFace { background-color: blue; transform: rotateY(180deg);
} .frontFace { background-color: green; transform: rotateY(0deg);
} .flipped .backFace { transform: rotateY(360deg);
} .flipped .frontFace { transform: rotateY(180deg);
}

We have two type faces. Normal and flipped faces.

Our important property is backface-visibilty: hidden;. This property helps us to hide back face of element.

Other important property is transform-style: preserve-3d;. This property gives 3D rotate effect to element when it is rotating.

And JavaScript file:

var card = document.querySelector('.wordCard');
card.addEventListener('click', () => { card.classList.toggle('flipped');
});

In above, we set click event to toggle .wordCard class. In this way, we could animate this card with transition duration.

Items.Title: 宣布我们的 Airtable x Webflow 同步！ | Announcing our Airtable x Webflow sync!
Items.Description:
今天，我们很高兴地宣布我们的Airtable x Webflow Sync公开发布！ 这是在 Airtable 和 Webflow 之间同步数据的最简单方法......时期。

在今天之前，我们的 Airtable x Webflow 同步已经进行了几个月的私有测试。 我们非常重视“打造少数人喜爱的东西”的理念。 感谢我们的早期用户，他们给了我们无穷无尽的宝贵反馈，我们终于觉得是时候为第一次同步揭开 Beta 版的面纱了。

以下是人们所说的一些事情

为什么我们决定建造这个
早在 2021 年 3 月，Curtis 和我正在构建一个前端工具——类似于 Stacker 或 Glide。 作为我们对该产品的用户研究的一部分，我们联系了数十名无代码开发人员，以了解更多关于他们的问题的信息。

打了几个电话后，我们发现了一些有趣的事情。 我们会从一系列关于无编码器设置的问题开始每次聊天。 他们会告诉我们一些关于他们的前端工具的信息，比如 Webflow 或 Retool。 然后他们开始谈论他们的后端，突然之间就像灯变了一样。 当他们向我们展示他们如何使用 Zapier 或 Integromat 尝试将数据从一个无代码工具复制到另一个工具时，他们的声音变得生动起来。 他们会分享在几周的时间里，他们如何将最终脆弱且有限的解决方案粘在一起。

在一次又一次地听到这个故事之后，我们意识到我们遇到了一个需要解决的真正问题。 那是 2021 年，距 SaaS 爆炸式增长已有十多年，但在您的应用程序之间共享数据仍然不容易。 因此，我们着手构建 Whalesync - 一种简单、无代码的方式，可以在 SaaS 应用程序之间真正同步数据。

为什么我们从这里开始
一个在所有应用程序之间同步的无代码数据工具需要一段时间才能构建，因此我们必须从较小的东西开始。 幸运的是，通过我们的用户访谈，从哪里开始的答案也变得显而易见。 虽然人们想要连接的应用程序有很多组合，但我们采访过的几乎每个无代码构建器都希望在 Airtable 和 Webflow 之间同步数据。

当然，如果您熟悉这两种工具，您就会知道这是一种自然的组合。 当您将两者联系起来时，您就解锁了构建令人难以置信的产品的能力。 例如，Create Better 和 Designer Tips 都是使用 Airtable + Webflow + Whalesync 构建的。

产品的作用
此版本使 Airtable 和 Webflow 之间的数据同步变得非常容易。 您选择要保持同步的数据表，将字段相互映射，然后打开同步。 从那里，Whalesync 将自动在 Airtable 和 Webflow 之间实时同步数据。 您更新 Airtable 中的记录，该更新将立即出现在 Webflow 中。 从本质上讲，它允许您将 Airtable 用作 Webflow 前端的后端。 它比任何自动化工具都更容易设置，并且开箱即用地处理富文本字段和多引用字段等内容。

我们下一步要去哪里
虽然这是令人兴奋的一天，但这只是一个开始。 我们的目标是让所有 SaaS 应用程序都可以轻松访问数据。 我们的 Airtable x Webflow 同步是实现这一目标的第一步。 虽然我们很高兴 Airtable x Webflow 现在可用，但我们正在努力尽快开放产品的其余部分。 在接下来的几个月中，我们将向 Whalesync 添加更多集成，并让更多需要这些同步的人离开候补名单。

感谢所有使此公告成为可能的人，但最重要的是我们的 Whalesync Pioneers。 我们很高兴能继续让产品变得更好！



原文:

Today we’re excited to announce the public release of our Airtable x Webflow Sync! It’s the easiest way to sync data between Airtable and Webflow... period.

Prior to today, our Airtable x Webflow sync had been in private beta for months. We take the idea of “build something a small number of people LOVE” very seriously. Thanks to our early users who’ve given us endlessly valuable feedback, we finally felt it was time to take the wraps off of beta for this first sync.

Here are some of the things people have said

Why we decided to build this
The idea for Whalesync was something we very much discovered rather than created. Back in March 2021, Curtis and I were building a frontend tool - similar to a Stacker or Glide. As part of our user research for this product, we reached out to dozens of no-code developers to hear more about their problems.

After just a handful of calls, we noticed something funny. We’d start each chat with a series of questions about the no-coder’s setup. They’d tell us a bit about their frontend tools like Webflow or Retool. Then they’d start talking about their backend and all of sudden it was like a light had switched. Their voices became animated as they showed us how they used Zapier or Integromat to try to copy data from one no-code tool to another. They’d share how, over the course of weeks, they duct-taped together a solution that was ultimately brittle and limited.

After hearing this story again and again, we realized we had come across a real problem that needed to be solved. It was 2021, over a decade into the explosion of SaaS, and yet it still wasn’t easy to share data across your apps. Tools like Zapier made it really easy to trigger an automation, but actually syncing data was a nightmare. So we set out to build Whalesync - a simple, no-code way to truly sync data across SaaS apps.

Why we started here
A no-code data tool that syncs across all your apps takes a while to build, so we had to start with something smaller. Luckily, the answer of where to start also became apparent through our user interviews. While there were many combinations of apps people wanted to connect, almost every no-code builder we spoke with wanted to sync data between Airtable and Webflow.

Of course, if you’re familiar with these two tools you know it’s a natural combination. Webflow is an amazing frontend website builder. Airtable offers a terrific database. When you connect the two, you unlock the ability to build incredible products. For example, Create Better and Designer Tips were both built with Airtable + Webflow + Whalesync.

What the product does
This release makes it really easy to sync data between Airtable and Webflow. You choose the data tables you want to keep in sync, map fields to each other, and turn syncing on. From there, Whalesync will automatically sync data between Airtable and Webflow, in real-time. You update a record in Airtable and instantly that update will appear in Webflow. In essence, it lets you use Airtable as a backend to your Webflow frontend. It’s easier to set up than any automation tool and handles things like rich text fields and multi-reference fields out of the box.

Where we’re going next
While it’s an exciting day, this is really just the start. Our goal is to make data easily accessible across all SaaS apps. Our Airtable x Webflow sync is the first step in making that a reality. While we’re happy that Airtable x Webflow is now available, we’re working hard to open up the rest of the product ASAP. Over the next few months, we’ll be adding more integrations to Whalesync and letting more people off the waitlist who need those syncs.

Thank you to everyone who’s made this announcement possible, but most of all our Whalesync Pioneers. We’re excited to keep making the product even better!
